content: I, Priya Mehta, wife of the deceased Arjun Mehta, residing at Bungalow No. 17, Sector 22, Kharadi, Pune, hereby file this FIR regarding the murder of my husband. On the night of 15th October 2023, at approximately 10:30 PM, I heard a loud scream coming from the backyard of our residence. I immediately rushed out and found my husband lying on the ground with a deep stab wound to his chest. A bloodied kitchen knife was found nearby. There were signs of a struggle — overturned chairs, broken glass, and blood spatter near the kitchen sink. I called 100 immediately. My husband had no enemies, but recently had a bitter dispute over property with his brother Rohan Mehta. I have no knowledge of anyone else being present that night. I have not left the house since returning from evening yoga at 8:00 PM. I am in shock and request immediate investigation. Signed, Priya Mehta.

I have been married to Arjun for 22 years. We had a quiet life. He was stressed lately because of the land dispute with his brother, but he never spoke of violence. On the night of the incident, I returned home from my meditation retreat at 8:00 PM. I prepared dinner, watched TV, and went to bed around 9:15 PM. I woke up at 10:30 PM to a scream. I ran out and found him dead. I did not leave the house after 8 PM. I have no idea who could have done this. The knife is ours — he always kept it in the kitchen drawer. I don’t know anything about the ₹25 lakh transfer. I swear on my children’s lives that I did not see anyone else that night.

I have known Arjun for 15 years. We were finalizing the sale of the disputed plot in Kharadi. The deal was settled on October 14th. I was at my office from 9 AM until 11 PM on the 15th. My security cameras show my car parked outside. I received a call from Nidhi Rao at 10:10 PM asking if the transfer was complete — I told her yes. I had no reason to kill Arjun. The land was legally mine. I never met him after 5 PM that day. I don’t know why someone would frame me. I have no idea about the DNA or the knife. I have never been to his house except for business meetings.

Arjun stole our ancestral land. The original deed from 2005 clearly names me as co-owner. He forged documents in 2018 to remove my name. I confronted him at the temple gate on October 15th around 8:30 PM — I saw him arguing with a man in a white coat, but I didn’t recognize him. I left because I didn’t want to escalate. I didn’t kill him. I was home alone all night. I didn’t know about the money transfer. I have no access to his phone or bank account. I only want my share of the property back. The police are ignoring my documents. Why is Vikram’s forged deed being accepted?

On the evening of October 15th, I was walking home from the temple after evening prayers. Around 8:45 PM, I saw Arjun Mehta standing near the temple gate, arguing loudly with a tall man wearing a hoodie. The man had a briefcase. I thought it was a property dispute — I didn’t get close. I didn’t see the man’s face. I told the police this in my initial statement. But later, I realized the lighting was poor and I may have confused him with someone else. I am sorry — I retract my statement. I was not sure. I didn’t see anything else.

I heard a loud argument coming from Arjun’s house at 10:45 PM. It sounded like two men shouting — one was Arjun, the other deeper. I looked out my window but it was dark. I didn’t see anyone. I told the police because I thought it was important. I didn’t know Arjun was dead then. I didn’t see a car leave. I just heard noise. I don’t know who it was. I swear I’m telling the truth.

I worked for Arjun for 4 years. I handled his phone, calendar, and financial apps. On October 15th, I left work at 6 PM. I was at home all night. I did not access his phone after 9 PM. I did not make any transfers. I have no idea about the ₹25 lakh. I don’t know who killed him. I didn’t talk to Vikram in October. My phone was lost last week — maybe someone stole it. I was scared after the murder, so I went to Goa to visit my sister. I didn’t run away. I just needed space.

I was assigned to record Father Thomas’s initial statement on October 16th. He clearly said he saw Arjun arguing with a hooded man at 8:45 PM. I typed it verbatim. Later, on October 18th, he came to the station and asked me to change his statement. He said he was mistaken. I told him I could not alter official records. He then handed me an envelope with ₹50,000 in cash and said, 'For the family.' I refused. I did not accept it. But I am afraid. I am not sure what to do now. I did not alter the original statement — it is still in the evidence locker.

I work for the Mehtas since 2019. On the night of the murder, I left at 8:30 PM. I saw Priya Ma’am leave at 9:45 PM in her SUV. She said she was going to a friend’s house. I didn’t see anyone else come or go. I heard no screams. I cleaned the kitchen before leaving. The knife was in the drawer as always. I don’t know about the money or the land. I am just a worker. I don’t want trouble.

I knew Arjun through yoga classes. We had an affair for 8 months — ended in September. I had no contact with him after September 25th. I have no idea about his death. I was in Rishikesh on October 15th. My retreat center has attendance logs. I have never met Vikram or Nidhi. I don’t know why anyone would link me to this. I am not hiding anything. My phone records prove I was not in Pune that night.

metadata.reviewed_by: Digital Forensics Unit, Pune Cyber Cell

content: CCTV footage from House No. 19 shows the following: - 09:45 PM: Priya Mehta exits her bungalow in a white SUV (registration MH-12-AB-7890). - 11:15 PM: Priya Mehta returns to her bungalow. - 09:50 PM: A white sedan (Vikram Desai’s car, MH-12-CD-4567) is seen entering the service lane behind Arjun’s bungalow, but the camera angle does not capture the entrance. - 10:20 PM: The same sedan is seen leaving the service lane. - 10:45 PM: No vehicles are observed near Arjun’s property. Footage timestamps were verified against NTP server logs. No evidence of tampering found. Note: The sedan’s license plate was partially obscured in the 10:20 PM clip, but vehicle model and color match Vikram Desai’s registered vehicle.

metadata.examined_by: Dr. N. Joshi, Senior Forensic Pathologist

content: Autopsy of Arjun Mehta (Age 48) conducted on October 16, 2023. Cause of death: Single penetrating stab wound to the left chest, severing the left ventricle. Time of death: Between 9:30 PM and 10:15 PM, based on body temperature, rigor mortis, and vitreous humor potassium levels. No signs of sexual assault or poisoning. Defensive wounds: Abrasions and lacerations on right palm and left forearm. Trace DNA under fingernails: Profile matches no database entry. Blood spatter analysis: Pattern inconsistent with struggle near kitchen sink — most spatter concentrated near backdoor. Murder weapon: Kitchen knife (registered to victim). Fingerprints on handle: Match Arjun Mehta. No fingerprints on blade. Blood on blade: None detected. Trace fibers on blade: Synthetic polyester, not from victim’s clothing. Conclusion: Weapon likely wiped post-mortem and planted at scene. DNA under nails is male, non-family, likely perpetrator.

Device recovered from victim’s study. Biometric login (fingerprint) enabled. Forensic imaging reveals: - ₹25 lakh transfer to 'R. Sharma' (Account: IB00123456789) initiated via UPI at 09:40:12 PM on 10/15/2023. Authentication: Fingerprint match. - WhatsApp logs: All messages from 10/10–10/15 deleted. Recovery via iCloud backup reveals 17 encrypted messages between Nidhi Rao and Vikram Desai discussing transfer, alibi, and 'cleanup.' - Fake screenshot of threatening WhatsApp message from Arjun to Vikram created using Arjun’s template and saved as 'land_deal.jpg' in Downloads folder. - Call log: One call to Nidhi Rao at 10:10:03 PM on 10/15 — duration 42 seconds — deleted from device, recovered via carrier backup. - Location history: Victim’s phone was stationary at home from 9:00 PM to 10:15 PM. Nidhi Rao’s phone (Android) was at her residence during same period, but cloud sync shows location spoofing via VPN app installed on October 14th.

metadata.bank: State Bank of India, Kharadi Branch

content: Transaction Summary: - 10/14/2023: ₹10,00,000 transferred from Arjun Mehta’s account to Priya Mehta’s newly opened savings account (A/C No: SBIN00987654321). - 10/15/2023: ₹25,00,000 transferred to 'R. Sharma' (Account: IB00123456789) via UPI. Label: 'Personal Loan'. - 10/15/2023: ₹5,00,000 withdrawn in cash from ATM (Kharadi Central). - 10/16/2023: ₹1,00,000 transferred from Vikram Desai’s account to Nidhi Rao’s account (labeled 'consulting fee'). Bank records confirm Nidhi Rao opened account on 10/13/2023. Priya’s new account was opened on 10/14/2023 with ₹10 lakh deposit — matching the transfer from Arjun. No prior deposits in Priya’s account since 2020. R. Sharma account is shell account registered under fake PAN. No physical person identified.

Original 2005 Deed: Names Arjun Mehta and Rohan Mehta as co-owners. Signed by both brothers and registered with Sub-Registrar, Pune. 2018 Deed: Presented by Vikram Desai to register transfer. Names Arjun Mehta as sole owner. Signed by Arjun Mehta. Thumbprint on signature: Digitally altered. Comparison with 2005 deed shows mismatched ridge patterns. Forensic analysis confirms 2018 deed was printed on paper manufactured after 2017, but the ink and signature were scanned from a 2005 document and digitally superimposed. The 2005 deed was found hidden in Arjun’s safe behind a false panel. Vikram’s legal team submitted the forged 2018 deed to register land transfer on October 14, 2023 — approved by Sub-Registrar on October 16, 2023, before fraud was detected.

Summary: Victim Arjun Mehta was murdered between 9:30 PM and 10:15 PM on October 15, 2023, at his residence. No forced entry. Weapon: Kitchen knife, planted post-mortem. DNA under victim’s nails matches no database, but matches Vikram Desai’s 2021 blood donation record (collected at Pune Blood Bank). Nidhi Rao’s digital footprint confirms communication with Vikram Desai and execution of ₹25 lakh transfer. Priya Mehta’s alibi is false — CCTV shows her leaving at 9:45 PM. Mrs. Kulkarni’s testimony about hearing argument at 10:45 PM is false — her porch light was off and her camera was disabled at that time. Father Thomas’s initial statement was recorded, but later retracted after cash payment from unknown source (Constable Ramesh has reported bribe attempt). Vikram Desai’s office CCTV was spoofed — original footage shows his car leaving at 9:50 PM. The forged property deed was created by Vikram using Nidhi Rao’s access to Arjun’s documents. Nidhi Rao fled to Goa on October 16. Motive: Financial gain through property fraud and elimination of victim. Suspects: Vikram Desai (primary), Nidhi Rao (accomplice). Priya Mehta may be complicit in financial fraud. Case forwarded for charge sheet.

IN THE COURT OF THE SESSIONS JUDGE, PUNE

Charge Sheet under Sections 302, 120B, 420, 467, 468, 471, and 201 IPC

Accused: Vikram Desai and Nidhi Rao

The accused Vikram Desai, along with Nidhi Rao, conspired to eliminate Arjun Mehta to gain control of disputed property and illegally transfer ₹25 lakh. On October 15, 2023, Vikram Desai entered Arjun Mehta’s residence via service entrance using a key obtained from Nidhi Rao. An argument ensued, during which Vikram stabbed Arjun. Nidhi Rao, acting under Vikram’s direction, transferred ₹25 lakh from Arjun’s account. The murder weapon was planted, and false testimony was orchestrated through bribed witnesses. Forged property documents were submitted to register land transfer. Nidhi Rao fled after the crime. Priya Mehta is under investigation for financial conspiracy. Evidence includes DNA match, digital communications, financial trail, and CCTV anomalies. Charge sheet submitted for trial.

JUDGE: Mr. Desai, you claim you were at your office until 11 PM. The original CCTV footage shows you leaving at 9:50 PM. How do you explain this?

Vikram Desai: Your Honor, I have a witness — my security guard. The footage must be wrong. My car was parked outside the whole time.

PROSECUTOR: Your Honor, the footage was tampered with. The timestamp was altered. We have the original file from the CCTV server.

JUDGE: And the DNA under the victim’s nails — matches you, Mr. Desai. You never had a criminal record, so your DNA was never entered. But you donated blood in 2021.

Vikram Desai: That’s true. I donated blood after my mother’s surgery. But I didn’t kill him.

JUDGE: The court notes the contradiction between your alibi and forensic evidence. Bail denied. Next hearing: December 10, 2023.
